title: IPv4 and IPv6 Static Routing
description: Configure static, default, and floating static routes on Cisco routers for both IPv4 and IPv6.
instructions:
## IPv4 and IPv6 Static Routing Lab

Configure static routes on two routers to provide full connectivity between all networks.

### Topology

```
[LAN-A]              [LAN-B]              [LAN-C]
10.1.1.0/24          10.2.2.0/24
  |                    |                    |
  Gi0/0    Gi0/1---Gi0/0    Gi0/1
  [  R1  ]------------[  R2  ]-----------[ISP]
  Serial link: 10.0.0.0/30              203.0.113.0/30
```

- R1 Gi0/0: 10.1.1.1/24 (LAN-A)
- R1 Gi0/1: 10.0.0.1/30 (link to R2)
- R2 Gi0/0: 10.0.0.2/30 (link to R1)
- R2 Gi0/1: 10.2.2.1/24 (LAN-B)
- R2 Serial0/0: 203.0.113.1/30 (link to ISP)
- ISP Serial0/0: 203.0.113.2/30

### Requirements

#### R1:
1. Configure a static route to LAN-B (10.2.2.0/24) via R2
2. Configure a default static route via R2 for Internet access
3. Configure a floating static default route via a backup link (next-hop 10.0.0.6) with AD of 210

#### R2:
4. Configure a static route to LAN-A (10.1.1.0/24) via R1
5. Configure a default static route toward the ISP (203.0.113.2)

#### Verification:
6. What commands verify the routes are in the routing table?
starterCode:
! ========== ROUTER R1 ==========
enable
configure terminal
!
! Step 1: Static route to LAN-B
!
!
! Step 2: Default route via R2
!
!
! Step 3: Floating static default route (backup)
!
!
end
!
! ========== ROUTER R2 ==========
enable
configure terminal
!
! Step 4: Static route to LAN-A
!
!
! Step 5: Default route to ISP
!
!
end
!
! Step 6: Verification commands
!
solutionCode:
! ========== ROUTER R1 ==========
enable
configure terminal
!
ip route 10.2.2.0 255.255.255.0 10.0.0.2
!
ip route 0.0.0.0 0.0.0.0 10.0.0.2
!
ip route 0.0.0.0 0.0.0.0 10.0.0.6 210
!
end
!
! ========== ROUTER R2 ==========
enable
configure terminal
!
ip route 10.1.1.0 255.255.255.0 10.0.0.1
!
ip route 0.0.0.0 0.0.0.0 203.0.113.2
!
end
!
! Verification commands:
show ip route
show ip route static
show ip route 10.2.2.0
expectedOutput:
R1#show ip route static
 S    10.2.2.0/24 [1/0] via 10.0.0.2
 S*   0.0.0.0/0 [1/0] via 10.0.0.2

R2#show ip route static
 S    10.1.1.0/24 [1/0] via 10.0.0.1
 S*   0.0.0.0/0 [1/0] via 203.0.113.2

Note: The floating static on R1 (AD 210) does NOT appear because
the primary default route (AD 1) is active. It only installs if
the primary route is removed.
hints: The default route syntax is: ip route 0.0.0.0 0.0.0.0 <next-hop>. This matches all destinations and acts as the gateway of last resort.
A floating static route uses a higher administrative distance so it only appears when the primary route fails. Append the AD at the end: ip route 0.0.0.0 0.0.0.0 10.0.0.6 210
Use 'show ip route static' to see only static routes, or 'show ip route' to see the full routing table. The S* indicates the default route.
learningObjectives: Configure network static routes with next-hop IP addresses
Configure a default static route as the gateway of last resort
Configure a floating static route with a higher administrative distance as a backup path
Verify static routes using show ip route commands
